Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and microalbuminuria, indicating significant renal dysfunction, possibly nephrotic syndrome or advanced chronic kidney disease. Metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂ and high chloride. Urine is abnormal in appearance and composition (protein, glucose, ketones), but without infection markers. Lipid profile is atherogenic. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended for further assessment and management.
